definition: The right knowledge that comes forth for one with right mental composure and belongs to the completed side of the path.
notes: MN 117 gives sammā-ñāṇa structural weight: for one with right mental composure, right knowledge comes forth; for one with right knowledge, right release comes forth. The repository therefore treats this as more than a document-local convenience. It belongs to the completion side of the path, where the trainee's eight factors open into the ten-factored completion of the arahant, the one beyond training. Right knowing can remain available when the local English benefits from a slightly more active noun, but right knowledge is the stable house default.

context_rules.context: In most path-sequence and doctrinal contexts
In prose emphasizing the active arriving of knowing rather than the settled noun
context_rules.notes: Allow this as a controlled alternate only when the context clearly supports it.

context_rules.context: When contrasting the trainee with the one beyond training in MN 117-style path exposition

example_phrases.notes: Shows the headword form in the tenfold completion sequence.

example_phrases.translation: for one with right mental composure, right knowledge
example_phrases.notes: Shows the sequence from right mental composure to right knowledge.

authority_basis.scope: Supports treating sammā-ñāṇa as an explicit completion-side path factor paired with sammāvimutti.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'right knowledge' as the default rendering in the entry's governed path-sequence and doctrinal contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'right knowing' only where a recorded context rule or unusually active English line clearly benefits. Do not flatten the term into generic insight-language or treat it as a decorative afterthought.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the factor from disappearing into generic knowledge-language or being detached from the tenfold completion sequence of MN 117.
